title: 8.0 Production and Post-production Information
text_content: Production and post-production information will be continually monitored and analyzed to determine if new risks have been identified and to review the assessments of known risks to maintain an effective risk management process. This information includes, but is not limited to, design and process changes, revisions of standards, identifications of defects, and reports of complaints. In addition to Varian’s information about the device, the following shall be reviewed: • Information on similar devices on the market; • Publicly available information such as recalls, FDA Medical Device Reports, and vigilance reports; • Scientific literature. Analysis of relevant data is used to demonstrate that risk-based decisions and risk control measures for this device are appropriate and effective. text_content: This analysis investigates all the aspect of the device from a bottom-up perspective. It identifies any inconsistency, design flaws and potential implementation failures. This FMEA concentrates on the investigation of: a) the architecture solution, its stability, adequateness, and flexibility b) the design and any components, its complexity, reliability, performance, effectiveness, criticality, and its implementation c) any interfaces within the components and 3rd-party applications d) any SOUP components e) Packaging, transportation and storage During the Analysis phase, if it is determined that a failure of a component / sub-system could also result in risk, an additional scenario is added to the RMR. This may result in additional hazard identification where top-down analysis did not identify potential hazards, but in most cases isolates additional contributory factors for existing hazards.

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

source: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report rev07.pdf
title: 4.0 Review of Risk Management Activities > 4.1 Implementation of the risk management plan
text_content: A comprehensive risk study has been conducted for HAL 4.0 in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 (1) and Varian’s Risk Management Process. The study was conducted by cross-functional teams consisting of the members identified in Table 1. The teams reviewed risk to health posed by all persons interacting with HAL 4.0. This analysis considered hazards that could occur both in normal use (or foreseeable misuse) and hazards associated with potential system or sub-system failures. Hazards, hazardous situations, and harms have been identified using SHaF (System Hazard Framework). The hazards, hazardous situations, and harms, applicable to HAL 4.0 are documented in HAL 4.0 Elaboration Phase RHA (10). The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations have been assessed and risk control measures have been identified. The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations as well as the risk control measures are documented in this report in Appendix B: Risk Assessment.

The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.2 Scope
text_content: This Risk Management Plan is defined for the HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 and all models of linear accelerators included in the platform. This Risk Management Plan documents the specific activities and processes undertaken to ensure the ongoing safety of this device throughout the product life-cycle. Potential effects of device hazards may impact manufacturing personnel, patients, operators, service personnel, bystanders and the environment in which the device is used (including interactions with other 3rd-party equipment).  All of these elements shall be evaluated as part of the risk assessment. Project risks such as schedule, budget, and resources are outside the scope of this RMP.

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).

This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. source: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report rev07.pdf
title: 4.0 Review of Risk Management Activities > 4.1 Implementation of the risk management plan
text_content: A comprehensive risk study has been conducted for HAL 4.0 in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 (1) and Varian’s Risk Management Process. The study was conducted by cross-functional teams consisting of the members identified in Table 1. The teams reviewed risk to health posed by all persons interacting with HAL 4.0. This analysis considered hazards that could occur both in normal use (or foreseeable misuse) and hazards associated with potential system or sub-system failures. Hazards, hazardous situations, and harms have been identified using SHaF (System Hazard Framework). The hazards, hazardous situations, and harms, applicable to HAL 4.0 are documented in HAL 4.0 Elaboration Phase RHA (10). The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations have been assessed and risk control measures have been identified. The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations as well as the risk control measures are documented in this report in Appendix B: Risk Assessment.

title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review
text_content: Risk management activities shall be performed per the Medical Device Risk Management procedure and work instruction (13). Each risk management activity shall be reviewed by persons familiar with the particular subject matter. Maintenance Plan Varian implements a product lifecycle framework that guides the incorporation of the Secure by Design principle in Varian products and solutions. The lifecycle framework also addresses the continuous management of cybersecurity risks after the products or solutions are released to the field. As part of the post-market cybersecurity maintenance, cybersecurity risk assessments are conducted periodically, or whenever it is needed in response to post-market cybersecurity signals such as 3rd party component vulnerability disclosure (e.g., critical Microsoft vulnerability CVE-2019-0708). Depending on nature and the level of risks, the results of the post-market cybersecurity risk assessments can lead to product updates, bug fixes, field services, Product Advisory, or Product Security Notification.

source: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report rev07.pdf
title: 1.0 Introduction > 1.1 Purpose
text_content: This risk management report (RMR) fulfills requirements of ISO 14971:2019 (1) by following Varian’s Medical Device Risk Management procedure. This RMR documents the review of the execution of the Risk Management Plan (6) for HAL Treatment Delivery Platform 4.0 (further referred to as HAL 4.0). Per the standard (1), an RMR shall at least ensure that: o the risk management plan has been appropriately implemented; o the device’s overall residual risk is acceptable; and o appropriate methods are in place to collect and review information in the production and post-production phases. The report shall be reviewed with the frequency stated in the RMP and updated whenever necessary to maintain complete and correct documentation of risks associated with the identified device.

title: 3. DAEN > 7.1.8 Conclusions of the Analysis of Manufacturer Data (Continued)
text_content: Mitigations through product design and labeling are in place. The mitigations were derived from thorough risk analysis processes through which risk control measures are formulated, implemented, and validated during product development. The risk control measures have been tested through verification and validation activities, and through product usability studies. The risks and their associated potential hazards have been assessed via analysis of available clinical data regarding the use of HAL. The safety findings in the clinical data were analyzed in context of the benefits to the patients as described above. Clinical data was analyzed for any evidence of the residual risks of HAL 4.0 identified in the RMR report. No adverse events or clinical toxicities were reported as attributable to the residual risks outlined in Section 5.1.4 (e.g. radiation over/under exposure, collision).
